_description: This file defines the Azure Fabric Semantic Model for the e-commerce project.
It maps directly to what you configure in the Fabric UI under:
Option A (Learning): Use this as a reference while manually
configuring the semantic model in the Fabric UI.
Option B (Advanced): Use the Fabric REST API or TMDL (Tabular Model
Definition Language) to deploy this programmatically.
WHAT IS A SEMANTIC MODEL IN FABRIC?
In Azure Fabric, a Semantic Model (formerly called a Power BI Dataset)
is a layer that sits on top of your mart tables and defines:
Business users never see the raw SQL — they just see the semantic model
in Power BI and drag fields onto their reports.
name: ecommerce_analytics
description: E-commerce analytics semantic model. Source of truth for all revenue, customer, and product reporting.

data_source.connection_note: Connect to your Fabric Lakehouse SQL endpoint. In Fabric UI: New Semantic Model → select your Lakehouse → select tables below.

tables.name: fct_orders
tables.description: One row per order. Primary fact table for all order and revenue analysis.

columns.name: order_date
columns.description: Date the order was placed. Use for time-series analysis.

columns.name: order_month
columns.description: First day of the order month. Use for monthly rollups.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Order status: pending, processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled, refunded.

columns.name: is_revenue_order
columns.description: 1 = counts as revenue (delivered/shipped/processing). 0 = cancelled or refunded.

columns.name: customer_segment
columns.description: RFM-based segment: champion, loyal, new_customer, at_risk, churned, one_time.

columns.name: acquisition_channel
columns.description: How the customer originally found us.

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Raw order total including shipping. Use net_revenue for P&L.

columns.name: net_revenue
columns.description: total_amount minus shipping. Primary revenue field.

columns.name: item_count
columns.description: Number of distinct line items in the order.

dax_measures.name: Total Revenue
dax_measures.description: Net revenue from non-cancelled, non-refunded orders only. Excludes shipping.

dax_measures.name: Order Count
dax_measures.description: Total number of orders including all statuses.

dax_measures.name: Revenue Order Count
dax_measures.description: Orders that generated revenue (excludes cancelled and refunded).

dax_measures.name: Average Order Value
dax_measures.description: Average net revenue per revenue order (AOV). Key e-commerce KPI.

dax_measures.name: Total Units Sold
dax_measures.description: Total product units sold on revenue orders.

dax_measures.name: Cancellation Rate
dax_measures.description: Percentage of all orders that were cancelled.

dax_measures.name: Revenue MoM Growth
dax_measures.expression: VAR CurrentRevenue = [Total Revenue] VAR PriorRevenue = CALCULATE([Total Revenue], DATEADD(fct_orders[order_date], -1, MONTH)) RETURN DIVIDE(CurrentRevenue - PriorRevenue, PriorRevenue, 0)
dax_measures.description: Month-over-month revenue growth rate. Requires a date filter to be meaningful.

tables.name: dim_customers
tables.description: One row per customer. Current state of all customer attributes and behavioral segments.

columns.name: customer_segment
columns.description: RFM segment: champion, loyal, new_customer, at_risk, churned, one_time, no_purchases.

columns.name: account_tenure
columns.description: new, established, or long_term based on account age.

columns.name: lifetime_value
columns.description: Total net revenue from this customer across all orders.

columns.name: days_since_last_order
columns.description: Days since most recent order. Used for recency analysis.

dax_measures.name: Avg Customer LTV
dax_measures.description: Average lifetime value across customers who have purchased.

dax_measures.name: Repeat Customer Rate
dax_measures.description: Percentage of purchasing customers who have bought more than once.

tables.name: dim_products
tables.description: One row per product SKU. Current catalog attributes and sales performance.

columns.name: total_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue this product has generated.

dax_measures.name: Avg Gross Margin
dax_measures.description: Average gross margin rate across products with sales.

relationships.name: orders_to_customers
relationships.description: Many orders belong to one customer. Standard fact-to-dimension relationship.

setup_instructions.step_1: In Fabric workspace, open your Lakehouse and confirm marts.fct_orders, marts.dim_customers, and marts.dim_products exist (run dbt first).
setup_instructions.step_2: Click 'New semantic model' in the Fabric workspace.
setup_instructions.step_3: Select your Lakehouse as the data source.
setup_instructions.step_4: Check the boxes for: fct_orders, dim_customers, dim_products.
setup_instructions.step_5: Click 'Confirm'. Fabric creates the semantic model.
setup_instructions.step_6: Open the semantic model → Model view. Create the relationship: fct_orders[customer_id] → dim_customers[customer_id].
setup_instructions.step_7: Click 'New measure' for each DAX measure defined above under each table.
setup_instructions.step_8: Save. Your semantic model is ready for Power BI reports.
